pi_items.source: Task description specifies coupon code 'SUMMER25'. Verifier code (line 196-204) validates code matching. Export script (line 36) searches for exactly this code. Schema verified against WooCommerce standard coupon post_title field.

WooCommerce official documentation confirms 'percent' is the standard discount type for percentage-based discounts. Verifier code (lines 206-217) accepts 'percent' as valid. Export script queries wp_postmeta meta_key='discount_type'. WooCommerce code reference and setup script (line 250) both use 'percent' for percentage discounts.

Task description specifies 25% discount. Verifier code (lines 219-234) converts to float and compares within 0.01 tolerance. Export script queries wp_postmeta meta_key='coupon_amount'. WooCommerce standard stores discount amount as numeric value without % symbol.

Task description specifies 'Usage Limit (per coupon): 50'. Verifier code (lines 236-250) converts to integer and compares. Export script queries wp_postmeta meta_key='usage_limit'. WooCommerce stores this as a numeric value in wp_postmeta. Schema verified in task_utils.sh line 71.

Task description specifies 'Minimum Spend: $75.00'. Verifier code (lines 261-277) converts to float and compares within 0.01 tolerance. Export script queries wp_postmeta meta_key='minimum_amount'. WooCommerce stores minimum cart requirement as numeric value. Database schema verified in export_result.sh lines 72 and task_utils.sh implicit field names.

privileged_info_summary: The add_coupon task expects a coupon with code 'SUMMER25', discount type 'percent' (25% off), usage limit of 50, and minimum spend of $75.00. All metadata values are verified against WooCommerce's standard coupon schema: discount_type is stored in wp_postmeta with value 'percent' (confirmed by official docs); coupon_amount stores the numeric value 25; usage_limit and minimum_amount are standard WooCommerce meta_keys for coupon restrictions. The task operates on synthetic data (local WooCommerce test environment with MariaDB), not real e-commerce data. The verifier script validates all five values with appropriate type conversions and tolerances.
